title: EEG: DPX Cog Ctl Task in Acute Mild TBI
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from a Dot Probe Continuous Performance Task administered to healthy controls and individuals with sub-acute mild traumatic brain injury (mTBI). Participants were assessed at multiple timepoints: within 3-14 days post-injury (Session 1), approximately 2 months (Session 2), and approximately 4 months (Session 3) following initial injury. The study investigates cognitive control mechanisms and attention processes during the acute and sub-acute phases of mTBI recovery. Note: Significant subject attrition occurred across timepoints. MRI data collection was planned but has not yet been uploaded.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a Dot Probe Continuous Performance Task implemented in MATLAB. Participants included healthy controls and individuals with sub-acute mild TBI, with assessments conducted at three timepoints: 3-14 days post-injury, ~2 months, and ~4 months post-injury. Data collection occurred between 2016-2018 at the Center for Brain Recovery and Repair at the UNM Health Sciences Center.
